question: Is a new NAS search on OFA-ResNet50 feasible for the 12.75 ms Nano budget @640?
finding: The SMALLEST OFA-R50 backbone (16.17 ms) already exceeds the whole baseline e2e (12.75 ms) — 1.81x the 8.91 ms backbone budget — before the pose head's >=3.84 ms. The entire elastic space (16-58 ms backbone) is over budget; no subnet fits. Mechanism: R50 is hardware-EFFICIENT (0.97-2.10 effective TFLOP/s, dense convs saturate the tensor cores, vs the MBv3 graft's ~0.30) but too COMPUTE-heavy (15.7 GFLOPs floor = 2.4x yolo11n's ~6.5). MBv3 lost memory-bound; R50 loses compute-bound. yolo11's own FLOP/efficiency balance is why compressing IT wins.
